purpose: Reference registry for the curriculum runner. Official standards and project documentation are canonical references; VeriSwarm docs are practitioner implementation references.

sources.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
sources.fit: Canonical framework for AI governance lessons, risk registers, control mapping, and executive review artifacts.

sources.name: NIST AI 600-1: Generative AI Profile
sources.fit: Use for GenAI-specific risk examples, evaluation controls, and governance module source verification.

sources.name: NIST SP 800-207: Zero Trust Architecture
sources.fit: Canonical reference for zero-trust architecture, trust boundaries, policy enforcement points, and identity-centered control design.

sources.name: OWASP Machine Learning Security Top 10
sources.fit: Canonical risk taxonomy for ML system threat mapping and mitigation prioritization.

sources.name: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
sources.fit: Canonical risk taxonomy for LLM security, agent security, prompt injection, insecure output handling, and tool abuse exercises.

sources.name: MITRE ATLAS
sources.fit: Canonical adversary tactics and techniques reference for ML attack walkthroughs and detection mapping.

sources.name: Kubernetes Documentation
sources.fit: Canonical reference for Kubernetes workload, network, admission, and runtime control behavior.

sources.name: Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts
sources.fit: Canonical source for supply-chain assurance levels, build provenance, and artifact integrity exercises.

sources.name: Sigstore Documentation
sources.fit: Canonical source for keyless signing, transparency logs, and verification workflow examples.

sources.name: OpenSSF Scorecard
sources.fit: Use for repo-health and supply-chain modules where learners need measurable open-source security signals.

sources.name: CNCF Cloud Native Certifications
sources.fit: Use to align Kubernetes-oriented curriculum paths with current CKA, CKAD, CKS, and KCNA expectations.

sources.name: VeriSwarm Trust Center
sources.fit: Use as an implementation example for agent trust, governance evidence, NIST AI RMF alignment, and tamper-evident audit concepts.

sources.name: VeriSwarm Gate
sources.fit: Use as a concrete reference for trust scoring dimensions, risk bands, policy tiers, event taxonomies, and decision gating exercises.

sources.name: VeriSwarm Vault
sources.fit: Use as a concrete reference for immutable event capture, chain verification, compliance exports, and AI governance evidence trails.

sources.name: VeriSwarm Architecture
sources.fit: Use as an architecture case study for hosted control planes, trust decisions, policy enforcement, and agent governance platform design.

sources.name: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689)
sources.fit: Anchor regulation for risk-classification (Annex III), conformity assessment, Article 9 risk management systems, and Article 73 serious-incident reporting. Use for EU-market-facing CAO scenarios and capstones.

sources.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System
sources.fit: Canonical AIMS standard. Use for control-mapping exercises, Annex A control walkthroughs, and as the certification target for governance-program capstones.

sources.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI Risk Management Guidance
sources.fit: Use alongside ISO 31000 for AI-specific risk-management lessons; pairs with NIST AI RMF for crosswalk exercises.

sources.name: OECD AI Principles (2024 update)
sources.fit: International policy reference for foundations modules; ties national frameworks (NIST, EU AI Act) to a shared principles vocabulary.

sources.name: OCC/FRB SR 11-7 — Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management
sources.fit: The bedrock MRM framework for US banking. Use for MRM-tier exercises, challenger-model lessons, and financial-services capstones.

sources.name: FRB SR 22-6 — Engagement with Crypto-Asset and Innovative Technologies (incl. AI/ML model validation expectations)
sources.fit: Complements SR 11-7 with current AI/ML validation expectations from the Fed; cite in MRM lessons and capstones.

sources.name: NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Regulation (incl. AI risk amendments)
sources.fit: State-level cybersecurity + AI requirements for NY-supervised entities; pairs with SR 11-7 for combined financial-services compliance scenarios.

sources.name: EU NIS2 Directive (Directive (EU) 2022/2555)
sources.fit: Incident-response notification timelines and supply-chain security requirements for EU essential / important entities. Cite in incident-response and compliance modules.

sources.name: GDPR Articles 33 & 34 — Data Breach Notification
sources.fit: 72-hour controller-to-supervisory-authority and data-subject notification rules; required reading for any incident-response capstone with EU data subjects.

sources.name: IEEE 7000 Series — Ethically Aligned Design
sources.fit: Family of ethics standards (IEEE 7000-7010) covering transparency, bias, well-being. Use for ethics module and as a counterweight to purely-compliance framings.

sources.name: California AI Transparency Act + SB 243 (Consumer-facing AI)
sources.fit: US state-level transparency obligations. Cite for jurisdictional-mapping exercises and consumer-facing-AI capstones.

sources.name: COSO ERM — Applying ERM to AI (2024 Supplement)
sources.fit: Bridges traditional ERM (used by audit committees + boards) with AI-specific risks. Use for board-reporting module and risk-appetite-statement exercises.

sources.name: Microsoft Responsible AI Standard v2
sources.fit: Worked example of a hyperscaler-scale RAI program. Cite in ethics + governance-program lessons as ONE implementation pattern, never as the canonical answer.

sources.name: Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy
sources.fit: Worked example of frontier-AI risk-tiered deployment policy. Cite as a practitioner pattern in trust-architecture and governance lessons.

sources.name: Google Secure AI Framework (SAIF)
sources.fit: Worked example of an AI security framework from a hyperscaler. Pairs with NIST AI RMF for security-overlay discussions in trust-architecture module.
